Our Heritage & Mission

A cultural institution in Musanze.

We're a registered non-profit working at the intersection of art, tourism, and community development — ensuring Rwandan culture empowers the people who create it.

Explore Departments Contact Us

How The Platform Works

Clear pathways from discovery to real community impact.

We designed the platform so visitors quickly understand where to start and how each action supports artists and local families.

STEP 1

Choose Experiences

Browse cultural and conservation tours with transparent trip details.

 STEP 2

Shop Authentic Art

Buy from curated Rwandan creators with clear product storytelling.

 STEP 3

Create Your Account

Track bookings, orders, messages, and notifications from one dashboard.

 STEP 4

Support Conservation

Extend your visit impact through focused community programs.
Our Story

Founded in 2012 to give Rwandan craft a permanent home.

Volcano Arts Center grew out of a frustration: the artists of northern Rwanda — the painters, weavers, sculptors, drummers — had no shared place to sell their work, host visitors, or pass down their craft to a new generation. Tourism revenue flowed past them. Galleries in Kigali took half the price. Most stories of Rwandan art were told by other people.

So we built one place. Today the Center hosts seven departments under one roof — an art store, an experience programme, a conservation arm, a talent pipeline, a partnerships desk, hospitality, and cultural education — all funded by the same business model: visitors come, buy, and leave a community better than they found it.

Mission

To make Rwandan art and cultural experience accessible to the world, while ensuring the people who create them are the primary beneficiaries.

Vision

A future where Musanze is recognised globally as a cultural capital — and where every visit funds the conservation of the volcanoes that brought visitors here in the first place.

By the numbers
14
Years
120+
Artists
7
Departments
Our Departments

Seven programmes of work, one shared model.

Each department has its own focus, leadership, and metrics — but they all share the same revenue mechanism: visitors come, buy or book, and the proceeds fund everything else.

Department · 01

Art Store

Our flagship: a curated marketplace for original Rwandan art — paintings, sculptures, woodwork, basketry, and the geometric Imigongo tradition unique to this region.

What we do

Source, photograph, and represent original works by named artists in Musanze. Each listing carries the artist's story; revenue split is transparent and tilted to the maker.

For visitors

Browse online with worldwide shipping (Stripe / MTN MoMo / Bank Transfer). Or visit the Center in person and meet the artist whose work you're buying.

PaintingSculptureImigongoBasketryWoodworkWorldwide shipping
Department · 02

Cultural Experiences

Half-day to multi-day programmes that put visitors inside village life — guided by hosts who grew up here, with capacity managed transparently and a clear refund window.

What we do

Design and lead immersive experiences: dance and storytelling evenings, traditional cooking with grandmothers, basket-weaving workshops, conservation hikes around Volcanoes National Park, and bespoke multi-day itineraries.

How it works

Book online, pay a 50% deposit (refundable up to 7 days before), arrive, and meet your host. Each experience has a managed group size — when a slot is full, it's full.

Dance & storytellingTraditional cookingVillage walksConservation hikesCustom itineraries
Department · 03

Conservation

Tree planting, water access, and education projects in the villages adjacent to Volcanoes National Park — funded by donations and structured around tiered impact.

How donations work

Donate one-off or set up a recurring contribution (monthly, quarterly, annually). Every donation generates a VAC-YYYY-NNNNN reference and a downloadable PDF receipt; we publish quarterly impact reports.

Active projects

Reforestation along the park buffer zone, school sanitation upgrades, and youth conservation education. Donors choose which campaign to fund.

ReforestationWater accessSchoolsWildlife bufferQuarterly reports
Department · 04

Talent Program

A structured pipeline for the next generation of Rwandan artists — bringing them off the street and into a professional system that pays.

How it works

Young artists apply through our online portal. Accepted applicants get a portfolio shoot, mentorship from senior artists, and their first paid commission. The strongest get represented in our store.

What we measure

Number of paid commissions, time-to-first-sale, and percentage of program graduates with sustained income within twelve months.

Apply onlinePortfolio shootMentorshipPaid commissionsStore representation
Department · 05

Tour Operator Partnerships

B2B integration for tour operators bringing groups to the volcanoes — partner pricing, group bookings, and a JWT-secured API for itinerary syncing.

What partners get

Authenticated API access (RS256 JWT), partner-tier pricing on experiences and group bookings, a dedicated portal, and a single point of contact at the Center.

Who we work with

Specialist gorilla-trekking operators, cultural-tourism agencies, conservation-focused travel brands, and educational tour groups.

Partner APIGroup bookingsPartner pricingOperator portal
Department · 06

Hospitality

A small, deliberately modest hosting operation — vetted home-stays and community guesthouses in Musanze for visitors who want to stay longer than a day-trip.

What we offer

Curated home-stays with families involved in the Center, traditional catering, local-led transfers from Kigali airport, and trip-planning support before you arrive.

Why community-hosted

Hotel revenue leaves the village. Home-stay revenue stays. Every booking through Hospitality circulates back into the same community network that runs the Center.

Home-staysCateringAirport transfersTrip planning
Department · 07

Cultural Education

Workshops, school visits, and our running blog of long-form essays on Rwandan craft history — keeping the knowledge alive and in circulation.

What we publish

Essays on the history of Imigongo, the symbolism of Agaseke baskets, the role of dance in Rwandan storytelling, and visiting-artist interviews. All free.

What we run

School visits in northern Rwanda, weekend workshops at the Center, and a running residency for visiting researchers and educators.

WorkshopsSchool visitsLong-form essaysVisiting researchers

Want to work with us?

Whether you're a visitor planning a trip, an artist applying to the Talent Program, or a tour operator looking to partner — start here.
